ProcedureName: CT Chest Abdomen Pelvis with contrast
ReportText:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: Bilateral adnexal masses. Elevated CA-125. Evaluate for malignancy and staging.

TECHNIQUE: Helical CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis performed after administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:

CHEST: Small bilateral pleural effusions, right greater than left. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal. No pericardial effusion.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: No focal hepatic lesions. No parenchymal metastases.
Gallbladder and biliary: Unremarkable.
Pancreas: Normal.
Spleen: Normal size, no lesion.
Adrenals: Normal bilaterally.
Kidneys: Normal bilaterally. No hydronephrosis.
Omentum: Diffuse omental thickening and nodularity consistent with omental caking, measuring approximately 4.2 cm in maximum AP thickness. Highly suspicious for carcinomatosis.
Peritoneum: Nodular peritoneal thickening along the right paracolic gutter and right hemidiaphragm. Small-volume peritoneal disease noted along the mesentery.
Ascites: Moderate free fluid in the pelvis and bilateral paracolic gutters.

PELVIS:
Right adnexal mass: Complex solid and cystic mass measuring 8.2 x 6.5 x 7.1 cm with irregular solid components and internal septations. Suspicious for malignancy.
Left adnexal mass: Predominantly solid mass measuring 5.7 x 4.3 x 5.0 cm. Suspicious for malignancy.
Uterus: Normal size with small intramural fibroid (1.3 cm). Endometrial stripe thin.
Bladder: Normal.

LYMPH NODES:
Para-aortic lymphadenopathy: Multiple enlarged nodes, largest measuring 2.3 x 1.8 cm at the level of the renal hilum.
Pelvic lymphadenopathy: Right external iliac node 1.4 cm (short axis), left obturator node 1.1 cm.
No inguinal or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.

IMPRESSION:
1. Bilateral complex adnexal masses (right 8.2 cm, left 5.7 cm) highly suspicious for ovarian malignancy.
2. Omental caking consistent with peritoneal carcinomatosis.
3. Peritoneal disease along right paracolic gutter and right hemidiaphragm.
4. Para-aortic and pelvic lymphadenopathy suspicious for metastatic disease.
5. Moderate ascites.
6. Small bilateral pleural effusions, no pulmonary metastases.
7. No hepatic metastases.

Findings are consistent with FIGO Stage IIIC ovarian cancer.

ProcedureName: CT Chest Abdomen Pelvis with contrast - restaging
ReportText:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST - RESTAGING

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade serous ovarian cancer, FIGO IIIC, s/p 3 cycles NACT (carboplatin/paclitaxel). Restaging prior to interval debulking surgery.

COMPARISON: CT 2025-09-20

FINDINGS:

CHEST: Previously noted bilateral pleural effusions have resolved. No pulmonary nodules. No lymphadenopathy.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: No new hepatic lesions.
Omentum: Marked improvement. Previously noted omental caking has significantly decreased. Residual omental thickening measuring 1.2 cm AP (previously 4.2 cm). Partial response.
Peritoneum: Previously noted peritoneal nodularity along right paracolic gutter and diaphragm has resolved. No measurable peritoneal disease.
Ascites: Resolved (previously moderate).

PELVIS:
Right adnexal mass: Decreased to 3.1 x 2.4 x 2.8 cm (previously 8.2 x 6.5 x 7.1 cm). Significant interval decrease.
Left adnexal mass: Decreased to 2.8 x 2.0 x 2.3 cm (previously 5.7 x 4.3 x 5.0 cm). Significant interval decrease.
Uterus: Unchanged.

LYMPH NODES:
Para-aortic: Largest node decreased to 1.1 cm (previously 2.3 cm). Near resolution.
Pelvic: Previously noted pelvic lymphadenopathy has resolved.

RECIST 1.1 ASSESSMENT:
Target lesions:
1. Right adnexal mass: 8.2 cm → 3.1 cm
2. Left adnexal mass: 5.7 cm → 2.8 cm
3. Para-aortic lymph node: 2.3 cm → 1.1 cm
Sum of diameters: 16.2 cm → 7.0 cm (56.8% decrease)
Response category: PARTIAL RESPONSE (>30% decrease)

IMPRESSION:
1. Excellent partial response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
2. Bilateral adnexal masses significantly decreased.
3. Near-resolution of omental carcinomatosis and lymphadenopathy.
4. Resolution of ascites and pleural effusions.
5. Patient appears to be a candidate for interval debulking surgery.

ProcedureName: CT Chest Abdomen Pelvis with contrast - post-treatment
ReportText:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST - POST-TREATMENT SURVEILLANCE

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade serous ovarian cancer FIGO IIIC, BRCA1+, s/p NACT + IDS (R0) + adjuvant chemotherapy. Completed 6 cycles carboplatin/paclitaxel. Pre-maintenance baseline.

COMPARISON: CT 2025-11-20

FINDINGS:

CHEST: Clear lungs. No effusions. No nodules. No lymphadenopathy.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: No lesions.
Omentum: Post-surgical changes. No residual or recurrent omental disease.
Peritoneum: No peritoneal carcinomatosis. No ascites.
Post-surgical changes in the pelvis and retroperitoneum.

PELVIS:
Post-surgical changes from TAH/BSO. Vaginal cuff appears normal.
No pelvic mass or fluid collection.
No pelvic lymphadenopathy.

LYMPH NODES: No retroperitoneal, pelvic, or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

IMPRESSION:
1. Complete radiologic response. No evidence of measurable disease.
2. Post-surgical changes from interval debulking.
3. No evidence of recurrent or progressive disease.
4. Recommend continued surveillance per protocol.
